Title: Create configurable bike product with single optional radio section
Preconditions: Admin logged in at ${BACK_URL}. Catalog module active. Storefront accessible at ${FRONT_URL}. B2B-mixed catalog exists and is linked to the storefront.
Steps: ADMIN STEPS - CATEGORY SETUP (prerequisite): 1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL} and log in as ${ADMIN}. 2. Go to Catalog > B2B-mixed catalog > Categories. 3. Click Add category. 4. Enter category name: Configurable Products [YYYYMMDD]. 5. Enter code: configurable-products-[YYYYMMDD]. 6. On the Properties tab confirm or enable configuration support (ensure the category allows configurable product type). 7. Save the category. 8. Verify the category appears in the B2B-mixed catalog tree. ADMIN STEPS - PRODUCT CREATION: 9. Go to Catalog > Products > Add new product. 10. Enter product name: Test Config Bike [YYYYMMDD]. 11. Set SKU: BIKE-CFG-[YYYYMMDD]. 12. Set base List Price to 200.00 USD. 13. Set inventory to 20. 14. In the product form set Category to the Configurable Products [YYYYMMDD] category created above. 15. Navigate to configurable/configuration tab. 16. Add Section: name=Choose Upgrade, type=Radio, optional. 17. Add option: name=None. Set list price=$0.00. Set inventory quantity=20. Save option. 18. Add option: name=Basic Seat. Set list price=$15.00. Set inventory quantity=10. Save option. 19. Add option: name=Premium Seat. Set list price=$45.00. Set inventory quantity=5. Save option. 20. Add option: name=Racing Seat. Set list price=$95.00. Set inventory quantity=2. Save option. 21. Publish and save product. FRONTEND STEPS: 22. Navigate to ${FRONT_URL} and open PDP for Test Config Bike [YYYYMMDD] (use direct URL or browse via Configurable Products category). 23. Verify Configure the parameters widget is visible. 24. Verify section Choose Upgrade shows all 4 options as radio buttons. 25. Verify None is selected by default. Price = $200.00. 26. Select Basic Seat. Verify price = $215.00. 27. Select Premium Seat. Verify price = $245.00. 28. Select Racing Seat. Verify price = $295.00. 29. Return to None. Verify price = $200.00.
Expected Results: Category Configurable Products [YYYYMMDD] created under B2B-mixed catalog. Product created with 4 options and assigned to that category. Configure the parameters widget appears on PDP. All 4 options present and selectable. Price updates correctly: None=$200 / Basic Seat=$215 / Premium Seat=$245 / Racing Seat=$295. Default selection is None at $200.

Title: Create laptop product with two required radio sections
Preconditions: Admin logged in at ${BACK_URL}. Storefront accessible at ${FRONT_URL}. Category Configurable Products [YYYYMMDD] exists in B2B-mixed catalog (created in TC-E2E-001). If this test is run standalone create the category first: go to Catalog > B2B-mixed catalog > Categories > Add category, name=Configurable Products [YYYYMMDD], code=configurable-products-[YYYYMMDD], save.
Steps: ADMIN STEPS: 1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL} and log in as ${ADMIN}. 2. Go to Catalog > Products > Add new product. 3. Name: Test Config Laptop [YYYYMMDD]. SKU: LAPTOP-CFG-[YYYYMMDD]. 4. Base price: $999.00. Inventory: 50. 5. Set Category to Configurable Products [YYYYMMDD] in the B2B-mixed catalog. 6. Add section: name=RAM, type=Radio, Required. Add option: name=8GB. Set list price=$0.00. Set inventory quantity=50. Save option. Add option: name=16GB. Set list price=$100.00. Set inventory quantity=30. Save option. Add option: name=32GB. Set list price=$250.00. Set inventory quantity=10. Save option. Save section. 7. Add section: name=Storage, type=Radio, Required. Add option: name=256GB SSD. Set list price=$0.00. Set inventory quantity=50. Save option. Add option: name=512GB SSD. Set list price=$75.00. Set inventory quantity=25. Save option. Add option: name=1TB SSD. Set list price=$150.00. Set inventory quantity=15. Save option. Save section. 8. Publish and save. FRONTEND STEPS: 9. Open PDP for Test Config Laptop [YYYYMMDD] on ${FRONT_URL}. 10. Verify both sections RAM and Storage are visible and marked Required. 11. Select RAM=16GB. Verify price=$1,099.00. 12. Select Storage=512GB SSD. Verify price=$1,174.00. 13. Change RAM=32GB. Verify price=$1,324.00. 14. Change Storage=1TB SSD. Verify price=$1,399.00.
Expected Results: Product created and assigned to Configurable Products [YYYYMMDD] category under B2B-mixed catalog. Both required sections visible with all options. Price math correct: base $999 + RAM option + Storage option. 16GB+512GB=$1174; 32GB+512GB=$1324; 32GB+1TB=$1399.

Title: Create configurable product with sale price option and verify strikethrough pricing
Section: Scenario 3 - Sale Price vs List Price
Preconditions: Admin logged in at ${BACK_URL}. Storefront configured to display both list price and sale price. Category Configurable Products [YYYYMMDD] exists in B2B-mixed catalog (created in TC-E2E-001). If running standalone create the category first: go to Catalog > B2B-mixed catalog > Categories > Add category, name=Configurable Products [YYYYMMDD], code=configurable-products-[YYYYMMDD], save.
Steps: ADMIN STEPS: 1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL} and log in as ${ADMIN}. 2. Create product: Test Config Sale Bike [YYYYMMDD]. SKU: BIKE-SALE-CFG-[YYYYMMDD]. 3. Base list price: $300.00; sale price: $250.00. Inventory: 30. 4. Set Category to Configurable Products [YYYYMMDD] in the B2B-mixed catalog. 5. Add section: name=Handlebars, type=Radio, Optional. 6. Add option: name=Standard. Set list price=$50.00. Set sale price=$40.00. Set inventory quantity=30. Save option. 7. Add option: name=Drop Bar. Set list price=$100.00. Set sale price=$80.00. Set inventory quantity=20. Save option. 8. Add option: name=None. Set list price=$0.00. Set sale price=$0.00. Set inventory quantity=30. Save option. 9. Publish and save. FRONTEND STEPS: 10. Open PDP for Test Config Sale Bike [YYYYMMDD] on ${FRONT_URL}. 11. Verify base: list $300 struck through; sale $250 shown. 12. Select Standard Handlebars. Verify: sale=$290 ($250+$40); list=$350 struck through ($300+$50). 13. Select Drop Bar. Verify: sale=$330 ($250+$80); list=$400 struck through ($300+$100).
Expected Results: Product created and assigned to Configurable Products [YYYYMMDD] category. Storefront shows list price with strikethrough and sale price as active price. Price math uses sale prices. Standard: sale=$290/list=$350. Drop Bar: sale=$330/list=$400.

Title: Verify that option sale price takes precedence over option list price in display

Steps: ADMIN STEPS: None required. FRONTEND STEPS: 1. Open PDP for Test Config Sale Bike [YYYYMMDD]. 2. Select Standard Handlebars. 3. Verify $400 struck through.
Expected Results: Sale prices always shown as active price. List prices shown crossed out. Applies to both base price and option-adjusted totals.

column12: TC-E2E-028 completed. Product Test Config Engraved Ring [YYYYMMDD] published at ${FRONT_URL}. Character limit on Engraving Text section is 30 characters.

Verify the browser does not crash or hang. 16. After all sub-tests: upload a valid 2MB-or-under PNG. Verify accepted. Click Add to Cart. Verify success.

Title: API-level add-to-cart rejects invalid and cross-product option IDs
Preconditions: TC-E2E-001 completed. Product Test Config Bike [YYYYMMDD] published at ${FRONT_URL}. TC-E2E-006 completed (Test Config Laptop [YYYYMMDD] exists as a separate product). Browser DevTools Network tab accessible.

Note the option ID used for Basic Seat. FRONTEND STEPS - ATTEMPT A (non-existent option ID): 5. Open DevTools Console or a tool such as Fetch/XHR replay. 6. Replay the captured add-to-cart request but replace the option ID with a random non-existent GUID (e.g. 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001). 7. Verify the server returns an error response (HTTP 400 or 422 or similar), NOT a 200 success. 8. Verify no item is added to the cart for this invalid option. FRONTEND STEPS - ATTEMPT B (option ID from a different product): 9. Navigate to PDP for Test Config Laptop [YYYYMMDD]. Select RAM=16GB. Capture the add-to-cart request and note the option ID for 16GB RAM. 10. Replay the bike add-to-cart request but substitute the laptop option ID (16GB RAM) as the option for the bike. 11. Verify the server returns an error response — cross-product option substitution must be rejected. 12. Verify no item is added to the cart with the mismatched option. FRONTEND STEPS - ATTEMPT C (missing required section): 13. Replay the add-to-cart request for Test Config Bike [YYYYMMDD] but omit the Choose Upgrade section entirely from the payload (send cart request with no configuration). 14. Note: Choose Upgrade is Optional on this product so this may succeed — document actual behavior. If the bike had a Required section, omitting it should return an error. Cross-reference with TC-E2E-007 which uses the Required laptop product for the definitive Required-section omission test. 15. Verify no server errors (500) for any of the above attempts — only structured 4xx responses.
Expected Results: Non-existent option IDs are rejected by the backend with a 4xx error response. Option IDs from a different product are rejected — cross-product option substitution is not accepted. Missing required sections are handled consistently per the product configuration rules. No 500 server errors are returned. Cart does not contain any items from invalid API requests.
